sourceMovie.title: A Most Violent Year
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: A Most Violent Year works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Most Violent Year clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Most Violent Year?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Donnie Brasco, The Bone Collector—each shares DNA with A Most Violent Year while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Most Violent Year?
faq.answer: A Most Violent Year (2014) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Most Violent Year worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
